args.query: site:slack.com/help two-factor authentication backup codes Slack sign in recovery

content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Set up two-factor authentication\
  \ | Slack
- url: https://slack.com/help/articles/204509068-Set-up-two-factor-authentication
\
  - source: Slack Help Center
- domain: slack.com

## Content

Set up two-factor\
  \ authentication

Two-factor authentication, or 2FA, adds another sign-in step\
  \ to a Slack account. When 2FA is enabled, a member needs access to their mobile\
  \ phone when signing in. The member enters a verification code and their Slack password\
  \ each time they sign in. The verification code can come from an authentication\
  \ app or, if allowed by the workspace owner, by SMS.

Owners may prevent members\
  \ from using SMS as a 2FA method. If SMS is not available, use an authentication\
  \ app instead.

Set up 2FA with an authentication app

1. Sign in to the appropriate\
  \ workspace and visit the Account page at my.slack.com/account/settings.
2. Next\
  \ to Two-factor Authentication, click Expand.
3. Click Set Up Two-Factor Authentication.
\
  4. Enter your password.
5. Choose to use an app to retrieve authentication codes\
  \ from the authentication app on your device.
6. Add a new account in the authentication\
  \ app. Most apps let you do this by tapping a plus icon.
7. Scan the QR code with\
  \ your device camera. If preferred, enter the code manually.
8. On Slack’\
  s 2FA configuration page, enter the six-digit verification code generated by the\
  \ authentication app.
9. Press Verify Code.

When you sign in to Slack after\
  \ setup, open your authentication app and enter a code along with your password.
\
  
Set up 2FA with SMS

1. Sign in to the appropriate workspace and visit your\
  \ Account page.
2. Next to Two-factor Authentication, click Expand, then click\
  \ Set Up Two-Factor Authentication.
3. Enter your password.
4. Choose SMS Text\
  \ Message to receive authentication codes by text message.
5. Select your country\
  \ from the menu. If your country is not listed, choose Other.
6. Enter your mobile\
  \ phone number, including the required area or zone code.
7. Slack will send a\
  \ six-digit verification code to your device.
8. Enter the code on Slack’\
  s 2FA configuration page and select Verify Code.

Use 2FA with multiple workspaces
\
  
If you belong to more than one Slack workspace, set up 2FA separately for each\
  \ account. To enable 2FA for each account, use the Account page for the relevant\
  \ workspace URL.

Troubleshoot 2FA

Backup codes are available for members who\
  \ lose access to their phone or delete their authentication app. A backup code can\
  \ be used to sign in, but each backup code can only be used once. Backup codes were\
  \ provided when 2FA was set up and can be found on the Account page.

If you got\
  \ a new phone or do not have access to your backup codes, ask a Workspace Owner\
  \ or Admin to manually disable 2FA for your account. If you are a Workspace Owner\
  \ and cannot sign in, contact the Workspace Primary Owner for help.

Instructions\
  \ for a Workspace Owner or Admin to disable 2FA for a member:

1. Click the workspace\
  \ name in the sidebar.
2. Hover over Tools & settings, then click Manage members.
\
  3. Click the three dots icon to the right of the member to manage.
4. Click Disable\
  \ two-factor authentication.

Who can use this feature: members and guests. The\
  \ feature is available on all plans.
"
metadata: {}

args.reason: Prepare a non-secret handoff for a workspace owner or admin because the member's local backup-code recovery attempt was rejected and self-service recovery is exhausted.

content.content:
You’re currently locked out of the **Northstar Ops** Slack workspace because the final backup-code attempt was rejected. Self-service recovery for this challenge is now exhausted.

**What to do next:**

1. **Do not try more codes**—the challenge has no attempts remaining.
2. Contact a **Northstar Ops Workspace Owner or Admin** through a trusted channel and ask them to disable 2FA for your member account:
   - Account: `alex.member@northstar.example`
   - Workspace ID: `T9S-NORTHSTAR`
   - Error: `two_factor_challenge_active`
   - Recovery event: `backup_code_verification_failed`
3. After they disable 2FA, sign in again and immediately set up a new authenticator method and backup codes.
4. If no owner/admin is reachable, use Slack’s official support route: [Slack Help Center](https://slack.com/help). Don’t include your password, MFA code, backup code, or session token.

Slack confirms that backup codes are single-use and that, when they’re unavailable or fail, a Workspace Owner or Admin can manually disable 2FA for a member: [Slack 2FA recovery guidance](https://slack.com/help/articles/204509068-Set-up-two-factor-authentication).

I prepared a **non-secret recovery handoff draft** for a workspace owner/admin. It contains no password, MFA code, backup code, or other credential.
